statement.markdown: Implement a TRIE data structure and fix the insertion edge case. The function should insert a string into the TRIE. Note that the string can contain special characters and spaces, but the string will always be valid and have a unique set of characters. If the string is a prefix of an existing string in the TRIE, the string should overwrite the existing string. If a non-alphanumeric character is encountered, it is treated as a special character. If the TRIE already contains the string, do not insert it again.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, the number of strings to be inserted. The following n lines contain a string each.
statement.outputFormat: No output is expected if the input is successful.
statement.notes: Time complexity should be O(n*m) where n is the number of strings to be inserted and m is the maximum length of a string. Space complexity should be O(n*m) where n is the number of strings to be inserted and m is the maximum length of a string.

constraints.description: Number of strings to be inserted

examples.explanation: Insert the string Hello into the TRIE.
Insert the strings Hello and World into the TRIE.

hints.text: Consider the insertion edge case when the string is a prefix of an existing string in the TRIE.
When a non-alphanumeric character is encountered, treat it as a special character.
Check if the string already exists in the TRIE before inserting it.

editorial.title: Fixing TRIE Insertion Edge Case Editorial
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to implement the TRIE data structure and handle the insertion edge case by checking if the string already exists in the TRIE and overwriting it if it does. Also, treat non-alphanumeric characters as special characters and check for prefixes of existing strings.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves creating a separate data structure for each character and then combining them to form the TRIE, which results in a time complexity of O(n^2*m).
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution can be achieved by using a more efficient data structure or by implementing the TRIE data structure correctly to handle the insertion edge case.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves implementing the TRIE data structure correctly and handling the insertion edge case by checking if the string already exists in the TRIE and overwriting it if it does. Treat non-alphanumeric characters as special characters and check for prefixes of existing strings, resulting in a time complexity of O(n*m).
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness proof involves showing that the implemented TRIE data structure and the insertion edge case handling are correct and efficient.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run involves creating a new TRIE, inserting strings into it, and checking for prefixes of existing strings.
commonMistakes: Not handling the insertion edge case properly
Not treating non-alphanumeric characters as special characters
